title: #marketing: 1746058921
preview:
Ben Carter: Kicking off the regulated workloads Vertical ABM pilot thread. Goal today: lock kickoff agenda, attendee list, and links so we can move fast.

Ben Carter: Proposed kickoff (45 min):
1) Pilot goals + success metrics (10)
2) Vertical choice + ICP hypothesis (10)
3) Asset list + owners + deadlines (15)
4) Target accounts + routing NA/EMEA (5)
5) Weekly cadence + reporting (5)

Ben Carter: Quick question up front: do we start with 1 vertical (finserv) for weeks 1–4 and keep healthcare as optional week 5+ expansion, or try to run both from day 1?

Camila Reyes: +1 to starting with one. We’ll lose time if we don’t get crisp on claims + evidence process.

Soojin Lee: Agree. Finserv is cleaner first pass on “regulated” without triggering HIPAA language landmines in week 1.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: From Security POV: start with finserv. Evidence pack + questionnaire responses are already closer to bank/insurance asks (SOC2 scope, audit logs, KMS, retention).

Naomi Feldman: +1. Healthcare messaging needs extra guardrails (PHI boundaries, BAAs, “HIPAA-ready” wording). Better to do it right, not fast.

Ethan Wallace: Works for content too. We can get 1 landing page + finserv one-pager over the line first, then fork for healthcare.

Ben Carter: Ok — consensus: finserv first, healthcare as phase 2 if we hit prep milestones by end of week 2.

Ben Carter: Attendees for kickoff (pls confirm):
- Camila (sponsor)
- Ben (pilot lead)
- Soojin (PMM)
- Ethan (content)
- Tyler (ops/attribution)
- Grace (SDR)
- Markus (SE)
- Naomi + Aisha (Security)
- Stephanie (NA sales), Lukas (EMEA sales)

Stephanie Nguyen: Confirmed. I’ll bring a view on which accounts are realistically reachable + who we already have warm intros for.

Lukas Meyer: Confirmed. FYI EMEA finserv list will be smaller but higher intent (a couple already asked about VPC).

Markus Klein: Confirmed. If we’re committing to “fast security turnaround,” we need a clear intake path for questionnaires + a default “evidence pack index” link.

Naomi Feldman: Yep. We should standardize: first response = FAQ + evidence index + offer 30-min security review.

Elliot Price: Docs side: I can join kickoff for 10 min. If we’re publishing landing page updates, we need review checklist + what we can/can’t claim.

Ben Carter: Perfect.

Ben Carter: Links / working docs (drop anything I missed):
- Internal brief (Confluence): /sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/vertical-abm-pilot-regulated-workloads-brief
- Finserv messaging framework v1: /sales-enablement/positioning-and-messaging/finserv-messaging-framework-v1
- Regulated workloads security FAQ: /sales-enablement/security-faq/regulated-workloads-security-faq
- Evidence pack index: /security-and-compliance/compliance-evidence/abm-pilot-security-evidence-pack-index
- Week-by-week plan (Drive): /go-to-market/abm-pilot-week-by-week-plan
- Target account list (Drive): /go-to-market/target-account-list-finserv-health-sheet
- Landing page draft copy (Drive): /go-to-market/landing-page-copy-draft-regulated

Tyler Benson: For metrics, I’ll use the spec here: /go-to-market/abm-pilot-metrics-dashboard-spec — will wire to HubSpot + UTM conventions. Need final CTA so I can set conversion events.

Grace O'Connor: For SDR sequencing, I’ll need final “approved language” block from Soojin/Security so we don’t have 12 variants floating around.

Soojin Lee: I’ll post “approved vs do-not-say” language after today. Biggest thing: no “HIPAA compliant” or “guaranteed residency” statements. We can say “supports customer-managed retention policies” etc.

Dr. Aisha Rahman: +1. Also no “zero data retention” as a blanket claim. We should be explicit that retention is configurable by deployment mode + contract.

Kira Thompson: I can take the action to make sure landing page links to the FAQ + evidence index and that wording matches Security. (We can keep it conservative and point to a live security review.)

Ben Carter: Owner callouts (initial draft):
- Messaging final pass: Soojin + Naomi (Aisha for final approval)
- Landing page copy: Ethan (review Soojin/Security/Docs)
- Landing page publish mechanics: Elliot + Kira
- Finserv one-pager: Ethan + Soojin
- Outbound email sequence: Grace + Ben (Soojin/Security review)
- Target account hygiene + routing: Stephanie/Lukas + Tyler
- Demo / pilot outline: Markus (with SE team)

Camila Reyes: Looks right. Add a “single source of truth” folder for what’s externally shareable vs internal-only.

Naomi Feldman: +1000. Evidence artifacts need restricted sharing. We should only share via approved path, not random attachments.

Ben Carter: Weekly cadence: proposing 30 min standup Tue 10:00am PT / 1:00pm ET (catches NA), plus async EMEA notes. Alternative is Wed 9:00am PT. Preferences?

Stephanie Nguyen: Tue 10am PT works.

Grace O'Connor: Tue 10am PT works.

Tyler Benson: Tue 10am PT works. I’ll post weekly dashboard update an hour before.

Lukas Meyer: Tue 10am PT is 19:00 for me, not ideal but doable short-term. If we can keep it to 25 min and do EMEA follow-ups async, ok.

Camila Reyes: Let’s do Tue 10am PT for the pilot, keep it tight. We’ll do a separate EMEA micro-sync if it becomes painful.

Ben Carter: Locked: Tue 10am PT standup. I’ll send the calendar invite.

Ethan Wallace: For kickoff, do we want the meeting to include a quick “what we’re *not* building” reminder? Helps prevent scope creep from security asks.

Camila Reyes: Yes. Explicitly: no major features; docs/packaging clarifications only. Anything else goes into roadmap triage.

Markus Klein: :thumbsup: Also request: 5 min on “fast path” for security questionnaires (who answers what, expected SLA). That’ll save us later.

Ben Carter: Added to agenda under governance.

Elliot Price: If we’re publishing landing page in week 2, please tag me early for the review queue. Last minute = risky.

Soojin Lee: Noted. I’ll keep wording conservative and route for security approval before it hits PR.

Ben Carter: Next steps:
- I’ll post the kickoff calendar + agenda doc in this thread.
- Soojin/Naomi/Aisha: drop approved language block by EOD tomorrow.
- Ethan: landing page outline + finserv one-pager skeleton by Fri.
- Tyler: initial dashboard + UTM naming by Fri.

Camila Reyes: :thumbsup: Thanks all. Keep this thread as the “kickoff command center” until we move into weekly standups.

Ben Carter: (reaction summary) Camila Reyes added :thumbsup: to Ben Carter’s message
Ben Carter: Cool — see you all at kickoff.

title: #eng-platform: 1745558642
preview:
Ben Carter: FYI seeing a weird spike in invoice-preview tokens for tenant `org-472` after yesterday's routing changes :thinking_face:
Ben Carter: looks like a lot of tokens are landing in the 'unattributed' bucket then getting reclassified later 
Jess Lin: ugh, is this the fallback->shadow path again? :eyes: any request_id samples?
Ben Carter: grabbed a few, here's one: req=01F8XQ9Z... route=primary -> fallback -> shadow -> meter.ended
Liam O'Neal: that chain rings a bell. earlier change to sessionization boundaries moved end-of-session attribution to the final consumer which means late-arriving kv-evictions can reassign credits 
Nayla R.: can we get the event ordering / ingestion window around those reqs? I'm worried the aggregator windowing isn't stable across retries
Ben Carter: query I ran (simplified):
```
SELECT req_id, route_path, event_time, token_count, attribution_state
FROM metering.events
WHERE tenant_id='org-472' AND event_time BETWEEN '2025-06-01' AND '2025-06-02'
ORDER BY req_id, event_time;
```
Ben Carter: results show duplicate token_count rows where a follow-up evt with attribution=unattributed arrives ~10-30s later and overrides earlier attribution in the preview
Sam: so invoice-preview (fast path) uses first-seen attribution but final ledger (slow path) dedupes/merges? that mismatch would explain preview spikes
Jess Lin: exactly. preview consumes streaming-agg snapshots, finalizer consumes join+dedupe. We changed the preview window to be more permissive last week for perf -> exposed this edge
Liam O'Neal: possible fixes: 1) tighten preview join window, 2) publish provisional attribution state to preview UI (mark as tentative), 3) add a short grace TTL for reassignments before preview tallies
Nayla R.: option 3 seems lowest risk to UX. implement a 60s 'soft lock' on attribution for preview only. if a later event attempts to reassigned within TTL, keep preview as-is but emit an anomaly signal
Jess Lin: agree. we should also add telemetry counts for 'reassign-within-ttl' so billing ops can triage tenants with noisy clients
metering-bot: alert created: MTR-REASSIGN-01 for org-472 (rule: reassignments > 5 in 5m). Incident link: https://ops.internal/alerts/MTR-REASSIGN-01
Ben Carter: can someone own a quick canary? I can ship a preview patch to add the TTL + anomaly counter to the streaming service
Sam: I can run a canary on shadow plane for a few tenants, will monitor invoice-preview dashboards and ledger diffs (use route=shadow tag)
Liam O'Neal: also add a per-request trace id in preview telemetry so we can link preview rows to finalizer rows. even just the req_id is enough
Jess Lin: ack. I'll update the console card to show a 'preview confidence' flag (green/yellow/red) based on reassign counts -> product wants not to surprise customers
Nayla R.: note for rollout: we need a migration plan in case TTL causes underreporting in short bursts. plan: enable soft TTL by default for 24h canary, compare total tokens over 24h vs baseline, then widen if stable
Ben Carter: + add a E2E test for replayed late events to prevent regressions. sample test: inject events out-of-order, assert preview delta < 1% after reconciliation
Sam: I'll run the canary today at 1600 PT. tagging @billing-ops on the alert and on the dashboard watchlist
Jess Lin: PR link once created: https://git.redwood/infer/streaming/pulls/2981 (will add unit tests + telemetry)
Ben Carter: thanks. if we see persistent reassign spikes after TTL, next step is to look at the fallback policy: maybe we should prefer last-known attribution from primary route rather than shadow overrides
Liam O'Neal: noted. also let's add a lightweight debug log line in the finalizer when attribution flips: `attribution_flip(req_id, from, to, delta_tokens)`
Nayla R.: and add a console card alert: 'Attribution flips > threshold' so support can proactively reach out to noisy SDK authors
metering-bot: canary deploy scheduled: streaming-preview-v2 -> shadow tenants (ids: org-472, org-512) at 2025-06-05T16:00Z
Ben Carter: cool. I'll follow up with a short postmortem thread if the canary shows regression. thanks all :thumbsup:

title: #marketing: 1745239876
preview:
Maya Ortiz: Quick check-in for NovaGrid case story — target publish next Wed AM. Need: one-line quote (text or 30s audio), logo consent, benchmark slide (95th pct latency + $/1M tokens), and webinar panel confirmed.

Ben Carter: I'll reach out to their PM for a short verbatim line. Ask for a percent but accept qualitative.

Olga Ivanov: I'll run the slide. Provisional numbers OK for the dry run, but add a short methods blurb: '4k context, steady-state synthetic load matched to customer traffic.'

Ravi Patel: Routing plan: registrants who pick 'Pilot' -> SDR-D; enterprise interest -> AE team. I'll add hidden field 'current_provider' to the signup. HubSpot tag: nova-launch-2026.

Maya Ortiz: Legal needs explicit consent snippet in the quote. Use: 'By providing this statement, NovaGrid grants Redwood Inference permission to publish the quote, company name, and logo.'

Ben Carter: NovaGrid responded: 'Redwood cut our inference costs and improved tail latency.' They won't share numbers publicly.

Olga Ivanov: Good to publish. I'll append 'anonymized metrics available on request' to the asset.

LegalBot: AUTO-CHECK: Consent clause missing in draft. Please upload final quote to /marketing/case-quotes and include consent. Logo permission required.

Ben Carter: Uploaded quote with consent line and logo.

Maya Ortiz: Owners: Ben (quote) done, Olga (slides + method blurb) due Mon 11am, Ravi (routing) done, LegalBot (consent check) pending final scan. We'll dry-run Tues 4pm.

LegalBot: Consent clause present and logo upload OK. No absolute performance claims found. OK to proceed.

Maya Ortiz: Thanks team — closing unless issues. :rocket:

title: #eng-runtime: 1742501234
preview:
Ben Carter: Uploaded profiling dump from this morning's A-load run. p95 up ~30% vs last week, p99 climbs when we hit microbatch=8. KV cache miss rate spikes in tail. https://artifacts/redwood/profiles/174250

Alice Nguyen: Can you paste the kernel-selection log? are we falling back to generic matmul at tail? :thinking:

Cheng Li: Quick summary: yes, we see kernel fallback after sequence len > 1024 + odd prompt shapes. batch=8 microbatching exposes head-of-line stalls. raw numbers:
```
- qps: 120 (stepped)
- gpu util: 78% avg, 95th burst 98%
- p95 latency: 180ms -> 235ms
- p99 latency: 420ms -> 710ms
- kv miss rate: 3% -> 12% at tail
```
Fallback log excerpt: `kernel_selector: no-opt-kernel -> generic_attention` repeated during bursts.

InfraBot: alert: gpu_util > 90% for group 'dedicated-prod' (3m window) at 1742502100 :rotating_light:

Ryan Patel: Two quick thoughts: 1) target_utilization should be lower for p99 sensitive routes. If our SLO is 200ms p95, cap util at ~70-75% for that route. 2) Admission control: throttle below headroom threshold, admit until predicted latency curve crosses SLO. We ran a simulation last month — I'll paste later.

Maria Gomez: +1 on admission control. Also consider graceful load-shedding with response hinting: return `cached_preview` when KV miss + queue depth high. Users prefer degraded quick reply to long tail. :thumbsup:

Cheng Li: Could also tune microbatch sizing per-request-class. For short-seq chat we keep microbatch=2; for long-seq batched jobs we allow microbatch=16. Proposed heuristic:
```
if seq_len < 256: mb=2
elif seq_len < 1024: mb=4
else: mb=8
```
But must guard with per-GPU headroom check: if util_predicted > 0.78 -> reduce mb by half.

Ben Carter: Good. Do we have profiling that ties kernel selection to seq_len distribution? If kernel selector picks fallback at >1024 we should add a special quantized kernel for that shape. Cheng, can you run a kernel sweep with quantized compiled kernel variant QK-v2?

Cheng Li: I'm on it. Running jobs with `--kernel-suite=qkv-v2 --microbatch-sweep 1,2,4,8,16`. ETA 2h. I'll post perf table.

DeployBot: Canary deploy scheduled: runtimes/kernel-qkv-v2 -> eng-runtime canary at 1742503000

Alice Nguyen: For the admission control prototype, let's add a simple PID-style controller in front of the queue that uses p95 rolling baseline. Example config snippet:
```
admission:
  target_p95_ms: 200
  headroom_util: 0.72
  action: shed/queue
```
Canary this on 10% traffic.

Ryan Patel: For load-shedding thresholds, suggest: shed when queue_depth > 20 AND predicted_gpu_util > 0.85 OR observed_p99 > 2x target. Keep telemetry so we can compute economic impact.

Maria Gomez: Also instrument KV cache TTL and shard hot keys. Right now hot key storms thrashed the LRU and amplified misses. Maybe add hot-key pinning for top-1000 keys.

Ben Carter: Next steps: Cheng -> kernel sweep + quantized perf table. Alice -> admission controller prototype + canary config. Ryan -> simulate headroom vs revenue tradeoffs. Maria -> KV pinning experiment. We'll reconvene when Cheng posts results. Thanks :raised_hands:

Ryan Patel: Two quick thoughts: 1) target_utilization should be lower for p99 sensitive routes. If our SLO is 200ms p95, cap util at ~70-75% for that route. 2) Admission control: throttle below headroom threshold, admit until predicted latency curve crosses SLO. We ran a simulation last month — I'll paste later.

Maria Gomez: +1 on admission control. Also consider graceful load-shedding with response hinting: return `cached_preview` when KV miss + queue depth high. Users prefer degraded quick reply to long tail. :thumbsup:

Cheng Li: Could also tune microbatch sizing per-request-class. For short-seq chat we keep microbatch=2; for long-seq batched jobs we allow microbatch=16. Proposed heuristic:
```
if seq_len < 256: mb=2
elif seq_len < 1024: mb=4
else: mb=8
```
But must guard with per-GPU headroom check: if util_predicted > 0.78 -> reduce mb by half.

Ben Carter: Good. Do we have profiling that ties kernel selection to seq_len distribution? If kernel selector picks fallback at >1024 we should add a special quantized kernel for that shape. Cheng, can you run a kernel sweep with quantized compiled kernel variant QK-v2?

Cheng Li: I'm on it. Running jobs with `--kernel-suite=qkv-v2 --microbatch-sweep 1,2,4,8,16`. ETA 2h. I'll post perf table.

DeployBot: Canary deploy scheduled: runtimes/kernel-qkv-v2 -> eng-runtime canary at 1742503000

Alice Nguyen: For the admission control prototype, let's add a simple PID-style controller in front of the queue that uses p95 rolling baseline. Example config snippet:
```
admission:
  target_p95_ms: 200
  headroom_util: 0.72
  action: shed/queue
```
Canary this on 10% traffic.

Ryan Patel: For load-shedding thresholds, suggest: shed when queue_depth > 20 AND predicted_gpu_util > 0.85 OR observed_p99 > 2x target. Keep telemetry so we can compute economic impact.

Maria Gomez: Also instrument KV cache TTL and shard hot keys. Right now hot key storms thrashed the LRU and amplified misses. Maybe add hot-key pinning for top-1000 keys.

Ben Carter: Next steps: Cheng -> kernel sweep + quantized perf table. Alice -> admission controller prototype + canary config. Ryan -> simulate headroom vs revenue tradeoffs. Maria -> KV pinning experiment. We'll reconvene when Cheng posts results. Thanks :raised_hands:

title: #architecture: 1739993210
preview:
Asha Rao: Heads up: planning a quick EU residency handoff drill next Thursday. Aim: ensure proxy-driven promote works without any non-EU writes. Anyone see problems?

Ben Carter: +1. Clarify: soft-affinity active/active path or emergency promote (active/passive)? We should test both but focus on the promote flow.

Asha Rao: Focus on promote flow. Soft-affinity still in place, but during node failure proxy should attempt local standby promote before rejecting writes.

Marta Ruiz: Residency rule recap: tenant writes must stay inside declared region unless explicit export flag set. If promote cannot complete, proxy must return error, not write remotely.

Diego Santos: Technical note: implement a short-lived lease with epoch fencing. Promoter sends fence request to control-plane: bump epoch, get confirmation. If confirmation fails within 20s, reject.

Ben Carter: Agree on epoch fencing. Also add header X-Effective-Region and X-Promote-Epoch to responses for validation and auditing.

Asha Rao: Test plan: flip feature-flag to allow local-standby-to-write, replay 2m of traffic for 30 tenants, assert: 1) zero non-EU write traces, 2) promote latency P95 < 800ms, 3) KV cache miss impact measured.

Marta Ruiz: Ops: avoid real VPC peering changes. We'll simulate promote with FF and mark writes with audit tag promote=true. ops-bot will create an incident if any non-EU write detected.

ops-bot: Simulation flag enabled for tenants: tenant-101..130. Alerting configured for non-EU write events.

Diego Santos: I'll add lease API: POST /v1/leases/{tenant} {epoch} -> 200 or 409. Proxy will require confirmed epoch to allow write path.

Ben Carter: Also gate metrics: promote_attempts, promote_success, promote_failures, blocked_writes. Dashboard widget pls.

Asha Rao: Owners: Diego = lease code + unit tests, Ben = telemetry + dashboard, Marta = runbook + ops config. Sync Friday to finalize. If no objections I'll schedule. :white_check_mark:

title: #eng: 1738960013
preview:
Connor O'Brien: pulling folks into one thread re: feature flag + cohort mechanics for onboarding checklist MVP. We need to lock this this week for rollout plan + Support readiness. PR is up (pr-28467) but there are open questions.

Dylan Brooks: thx. current implementation in Console uses `onboarding_checklist_enabled` from config service. if false, we hide entrypoint + don’t call progress API. if true, UI loads and renders whatever state.

Chloe Martin: backend side (progress svc) is always on in prod (since gateway can emit events regardless). but we can gate the *Console access* + step details. I’m +1 to not gating event emission; otherwise we create data gaps.

Logan Wright: +1. telemetry wants events regardless so we can baseline funnel / TTFS pre/post. gating event emission by flag breaks comparability. we can add `flag_variant`/`exposure` as context in the events if we need.

Ben Carter: for cohorting, we should decide: tenant-level or user-level. Product asked for “accounts see checklist” not “some users in same org see different things”. I strongly recommend tenant-level.

Marissa Cole: tenant-level is the intent. we don’t want a support scenario where admin sees checklist and other teammate doesn’t.

Nikhil Sharma: agree. also for TTFS/TTFP reporting we care per tenant, so exposure should be stable.

Connor O'Brien: ok tenant-level. question: where do we compute cohort? console side vs server side.

Ethan Park: compute server-side in control plane/config. if Console computes cohort locally you can get drift (different SDK versions, different hashing). Better: config service returns `onboardingChecklist: { enabled: true, variant: "A", cohort: "dogfood" }`.

Dylan Brooks: today we’re calling `GET /v1/console/config` and it returns feature gates already. we can extend.

Talia Benson: also helps with “kill switch” - one place to flip.

Connor O'Brien: cool. what’s the cohort rule? what fields do we use for deterministic bucketing.

Ben Carter: do NOT use email domain. use `tenant_id` stable hash. segmentation rules layered on top:
1) allowlist internal dogfood tenants
2) allowlist a few design partners
3) then % rollout by tenant_id hash bucket
We can also exclude “enterprise” plan if we want, but we need clarity.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE perspective, % rollout must be a real kill switch (0%) and must be fast to propagate (<5 min). also: if there’s an outage in progress svc, the flag should fail closed (hide UI) not degrade console performance.

Chloe Martin: progress svc is behind the same edge as Console API. if it errors, UI can show “Checklist temporarily unavailable” and still render rest of Console. We can also have Console only call it if enabled.

Allison Grant: we should treat it as non-critical path. timeouts should be low (e.g., 300-500ms) + no retries from browser. avoid cascading issues.

Dylan Brooks: yup, I set 400ms timeout and on failure we drop the component (no blocking). we still log a metric + Sentry event.

Nadia Rahman: re: adding `flag_variant` to events: prefer server-side enrichment. gateway emits events; telemetry pipeline can join against exposure dataset keyed by tenant_id + timestamp. otherwise you leak flag evaluation details into multiple emitters.

Logan Wright: +1. we can write an “exposure snapshot” table in analytics daily + also a real-time KV for joins. but simplest: config svc also emits `onboarding.checklist.exposed` event when enabled flips from false->true for a tenant.

Connor O'Brien: do we need an explicit exposure event for counting? or can we just use config snapshot.

Logan Wright: for accurate funnel denominators, explicit exposure event is nice. but we can ship MVP with snapshot and add event later.

Marissa Cole: I’m ok with snapshot for MVP as long as we can answer “who saw the checklist” reliably. Support will ask.

Amara Diallo: question: if we don’t gate gateway event emission, do we risk “step complete” happening for tenants who never saw checklist? ex: api key created, first 2xx request, etc.

Chloe Martin: that’s fine. progress state is basically computed from behavior; showing UI is separate. if tenant later gets exposed, their checklist will appear partially complete (which might be good!).

Ben Carter: agree. and it reduces “cold start” confusion.

Nikhil Sharma: BUT: need to make sure step ordering doesn’t look weird if they already did prod stuff. e.g., “Set budget alerts” might be incomplete while “Send production traffic” looks complete.

Dylan Brooks: UX spec has “Recommended next” behavior. If some advanced steps are complete, we still show sequence but highlight the first incomplete. Also can collapse completed steps.

Talia Benson: ok. rollout mechanics: what cohorts for phase 1 dogfood?

Connor O'Brien: proposal:
- Week 1 internal (Redwood tenants) + 3 handpicked partner tenants
- Then 5% new self-serve signups (created_at >= rollout date)
- Then 25% all eligible
Need to decide if we only target *new* tenants or include existing.

Ben Carter: for activation lift we care mainly new. I’d do new-only initially to avoid surprising existing customers in Console. But telemetry wants some existing for baseline comparisons.

Logan Wright: baseline comparisons don’t require exposure; we can compare pre vs post on new signups only. I’m ok with new-only for the UI.

Marissa Cole: new-only simplifies Support training too (“If they signed up after X they probably have it”).

Sean Gallagher: new-only reduces blast radius. do it.

Chloe Martin: implementing “new-only” requires we have tenant.created_at in config service. do we already have that accessible?

Ethan Park: yes in control plane. config svc can evaluate rule `created_at >= 2025-02-xx`.

Dylan Brooks: one nuance: if we gate on created_at, tenants created just before date never get it unless we run a migration/override. maybe we want an allowlist for “requested access”.

Connor O'Brien: we can include an override list. Support asked for this anyway (ENG-5852 admin override is separate though). For rollout gating, we can maintain a “rollout allowlist” in config.

Ben Carter: yes. allowlist should be in control plane, editable by oncall/Release coord.

Allison Grant: please put guardrails: any manual allowlist change should be audited. doesn’t need full SOC2 today but at least who/when.

Ethan Park: we can log changes in config admin logs.

LaunchDarkly Bot: LaunchDarkly flag update: `console.onboarding_checklist` created in project `redwood-console`. Default: off. Rules: allowlist (internal tenants), then 0% rollout.

Dylan Brooks: wait we’re using LaunchDarkly directly? I thought we were keeping feature flags in control plane config.

Connor O'Brien: sorry, to clarify: pr-28467 uses LD SDK server-side in config service. Console reads config service, not LD directly.

Chloe Martin: ok that’s fine.

Nadia Rahman: if LD is used, please ensure config service caches evaluation results (per tenant) w/ TTL. don’t want every console page load hammering LD.

Ethan Park: caching is in place. We cache per tenant_id for 5 minutes; bust cache on config change webhook.

Sean Gallagher: 5 minutes ok. make sure kill switch propagation meets SRE ask. webhook path must be reliable.

Ethan Park: webhook already used for other flags. if webhook fails, worst case TTL 5 min.

Connor O'Brien: decision points to record:
1) tenant-level bucketing
2) config service evaluates flag (LD backed)
3) UI gated, event emission not gated
4) new tenants only initially + allowlist override
5) fail closed if progress svc down (hide component)
Anything else?

Marissa Cole: also: exclude enterprise contracts at first? I’d exclude anything with `plan_tier=enterprise` until Support + CS trained.

Ben Carter: I’d exclude enterprise by default, but allowlist for design partners.

Connor O'Brien: ok. Ethan can add rule in config service.

Dylan Brooks: I’ll update Console to display a small “Beta” pill only for dogfood cohort? or for all. UX spec says avoid “beta” unless we think it’s unstable.

Nikhil Sharma: avoid beta label; it makes customers nervous. use normal UI.

Talia Benson: timeline: can we land the rule changes today so we can start dogfood tomorrow?

Ethan Park: yes if decisions final. I’ll update config rules + add fields returned to Console: `enabled`, `cohort`, `rollout_reason` (for debugging; not shown in UI).

Allison Grant: careful with `rollout_reason` surfacing to client. don’t leak internal segmentation logic. keep it server logs only.

Dylan Brooks: +1. we can keep it behind an internal header or only in logs.

Connor O'Brien: ok. action items:
- Ethan: implement new-only + enterprise exclude + allowlist; keep reason server-side
- Dylan: ensure progress API calls are gated + timeout/fail closed + no retries
- Logan/Nadia: confirm telemetry approach for exposure denominator (snapshot ok MVP)
- Connor: update PM-768 + rollout doc with these decisions

Logan Wright: I’ll post in #eng-platform that telemetry is ok with snapshot for MVP, but we should add an exposure event in phase 2.

Sean Gallagher: and please define kill criteria. If error rate for progress svc > X or console p95 increases by Y, we flip to 0%.

Connor O'Brien: yep will add go/no-go + kill switch thresholds in rollout plan.

Marissa Cole: thanks all. once config is updated, ping me and I’ll sanity check in staging.

Dylan Brooks: will do. :thumbsup:

title: #incidents: 1738501234
preview:
pagerbot: :rotating_light: PagerDuty: page fired for service=embeddings-api-prod (High 5xx rate)
pagerbot: Incident ID: INC-2026-2243 — auto-created and alerted to on-call rotation -> primary: Alice Nguyen
Alice Nguyen: acked. I'm on it, looking at graphs now. cc @Carla Mendez in case we need escalation.
Ben Carter: i'm seeing a big spike on eu-prod region, 5xx rate up to 9% on /v1/embeddings. latency stable. any rollout recently?
Alice Nguyen: not that I know of, checking deployment history + release notes.
DevOpsBot: deploybot: no recent rollout in the last 6 hours for embeddings-svc.
Alice Nguyen: ok, running quick checks — kubectl exec to tail logs on one pod, and hitting health endpoints.
Alice Nguyen: ```
kubectl -n inference get pods -l app=embeddings-svc -o wide
kubectl -n inference logs -f deployment/embeddings-svc --tail=200 | grep ERROR | head -n 50
curl -sS https://eu-embeddings.prod/health || echo failed
```
Carla Mendez: I'm on rotation backup. If this is region-specific we should consider routing to us-west fallback while we triage.
Ben Carter: logs show kvcache eviction spikes and downstream GRPC 502 from kvstore cluster-3. Could be the embedded cache node flapping.
Alice Nguyen: good catch. Paging seems to have fired multiple times though — I got duplicate PD pages from pagerbot (same incident) then separate alert for "embeddings-queue" rule. Paging flood is noisy.
SRE Lead: noted — looks like two alert rules (5xx-rate + queue-latency) are firing at same time and no dedup. I'll mute the secondary rule to reduce noise.
SRE Lead: muting rule: alerts/embeddings-queue-latency for 30m while we investigate.
pagerbot: :white_check_mark: Muted alerts/embeddings-queue-latency (30m) per SRE Lead
Maya (CS): customer impact — CS dashboard shows ~12 customers reported errors in last 30m, highest severity for customers using batch-embed job. We should prepare a banner.
Carla Mendez: agreed. Can we flip to cached-embeddings fallback for batch jobs? there's a feature flag we can toggle.
Ben Carter: feature flag is remote-config key: embeddings.cache_fallback=true. I can toggle but need approval for potential staleness.
Alice Nguyen: do it. set TTL to 24h and put a note in incident. Toggle now and watch error rate.
Ben Carter: toggled. ```
# via remote-config
set_key embeddings.cache_fallback true --ttl=24h --note "incident INC-2026-2243 fallback"
```
DevOpsBot: rollout triggered: restarting 3/3 embeddings-svc pods to pick up config.
Ben Carter: after toggle + restart error rate down to 1.6% in eu-prod, queue backlog falling. Good signal.
Alice Nguyen: handing ownership to Carla for next 2h to monitor region failover and customer comms. Carla — you okay taking lead?
Carla Mendez: taking it. I'll coordinate with CS on banner and prepare postmortem checklist.
Maya (CS): prepping banner now: "We are seeing intermittent failures for embeddings in EU region. We're routing to fallback and monitoring. No action required from customers." will publish in 5m.
SRE Lead: follow-up actions: 1) Investigate kvstore cluster-3 flapping nodes (Ben). 2) Add dedup rule in alerting to group 5xx + queue-latency into single incident or use correlation key (SRE Lead). 3) Run capacity test on kvcache (Alice + infra). 4) Postmortem assign to Carla.
Carla Mendez: noted. I'll open incident doc and link runbook: https://redwood.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ENG/pages/Embeddings+Incident+Runbook
Alice Nguyen: thanks team. I'll keep monitoring traces for any regression and will ping here if anything changes.
pagerbot: :information_source: Incident INC-2026-2243 ownership transferred to Carla Mendez
Carla Mendez: status ping — 15m since mitigation, 5xx rate steady ~1.5%, backlog decreased 78%. continuing to monitor, will update in 20m or on any change.
Maya (CS): banner posted. CS triage queue open for customers that escalate.
Ben Carter: note to self — plan for adding circuit breaker on kvstore GRPC calls and better retry/backoff.
SRE Lead: agreed. I'll schedule a short follow-up meeting tomorrow to decide long-term changes and alert consolidation.
Carla Mendez: closing the noisy pages and following the runbook. Will publish a short incident summary after we validate root cause.
Alice Nguyen: +1 — thanks everyone. :heart:

title: #marketing: 1732201123
preview:
Maya Kapoor: Drafting a one-pager for enterprise buyers (security + SLA + deployment modes). Needs: one-page PDF, compact website block, and webinar. Keep copy tight for procurement teams. Any must-haves?

Ben Carter: Headline idea: 'Secure, SLA-backed LLM inference across Hosted, Dedicated, and Private deployments.' Then 3 short bullets per pillar and one benchmark callout.

Samir Gupta: Routing rules: route to AE if compliance_needed == true OR annual_commit >= 50k. Otherwise self-serve with sales opt-in. Add fields: deployment_preference, compliance_needed, expected_token_volume. Example lead JSON: `\{\"deployment_preference\":\"Hosted|Dedicated|Private\",\"compliance_needed\":true,\"expected_token_volume\":\"number\"\}`

Lena Fox: Legal check: wording must be careful. Say "supports customer-managed KMS, VPC/private control plane, and audit logs." For SLAs use "SLA-backed uptime subject to standard exclusions (scheduled maintenance, force majeure)." Do not mention certifications unless validated.

Devon Park: Benchmark blurb suggestion: "Internal lab tests: Dedicated + quantization profiles reduced unit cost by ~20-35% at sustained high throughput; typical p99 gen latency observed 110-150ms depending on model." Call out workload: embeddings + reranking. Keep "internal tests" qualifier.

Iris Novak: Webinar tie-in: target Dec 2, 30-min (20-min demo + 10-min Q&A). Dry run Dec 1. Promo channels: blog, targeted emails to trial users, in-product banner. I'll draft the registration page copy.

Maya Kapoor: Nice. For the one-pager copy I'm thinking 3 micro-headlines: Security & Data Controls, SLA & Reliability, Deployment Flexibility. Each gets 3 bullets, one benchmark callout box, and a single CTA button: either "Request enterprise trial" or "Schedule secure demo" (we'll A/B test).

Ben Carter: Visuals: small icons (shield, clock, cloud with lock) + tiny chart for cost per 1M tokens (Hosted vs Dedicated vs Self-host). Keep PDF one-column.

Samir Gupta: AE playbook bullets: typical contracting timeline 30-60 days for Dedicated + security review; required docs: DPA, network diagram, PO/contract. Who to loop: Solutions Eng, Security.

Lena Fox: I'll run the final copy for legal sign-off before publish. Also add a short compliance checklist block: encryption at rest, customer KMS, HSM option, audit logs, VPC peering.

Devon Park: I'll prepare two short benchmark callouts (cost and latency) for the hero box and the webinar slide. Keep numbers rounded and qualified.

Maya Kapoor: Action items: @Samir - lead routing + JSON form fields; @Lena - legal phrasing; @Devon - benchmark blurbs + slides; @Iris - webinar reg copy; @Ben - hero mock. I'll assemble draft one-pager and circulate by EOD tomorrow. Meet Thu 9AM to finalize.

title: #eng-platform: 1723456789
preview:
Maya Patel: Quick question — our canary dashboard currently shows p50/p95/p99 comparisons vs baseline, but it's hard to tell significance for low-traffic routes. Can we add a 'stat sig' column and a drilldown that shows per-customer slices?
Jorge Ramos: +1, especially for routes that only see a few qps. Right now the alert triggers spam on small samples.
Sasha Li: We could compute bootstrapped CIs and mark metrics as 'insufficient data' under N<100 requests.
Ben Carter: I like that. Also want a way to exclude canary traffic under quota throttling so the split math isn't skewed.
Maya Patel: Thinking UX: in the rollout page, show canary vs baseline, delta, CI, and a small sparkline. Clicking delta opens a side panel with breakdown by region, route, customer-tier.
Jorge Ramos: For alerts, we should support 'absolute' and 'relative' guards. Relative uses percent delta > x AND statistically significant. Absolute for latency > y ms.
Sasha Li: Sample alert config idea:
```
alert: canary_regression
metric: inference_latency_p95
compare_to: baseline
min_samples: 200
relative_threshold: 1.15 # 15% worse
require_significance: true
window: 10m
```
Ben Carter: We'll need to wire this to the existing alerts service. Can we surface the exact query used for each metric so SREs can reproduce?
Maya Patel: Yeah — include a 'view query' link that opens the PromQL/Analytics query in a new tab.
deploy-bot: deploy completed for rollout/123 canary=10% at 2024-05-12T11:12:34Z
Jorge Ramos: Also add a 'traffic-adjusted baseline' option — if baseline saw a change in traffic composition, compare with matched cohorts.
Sasha Li: Matching cohorts is heavier compute. Maybe do it on demand for the drilldown panel only.
Ben Carter: For the dashboard, a table row per metric and on-hover shows delta significance p-value. Use color coding: red for regression, yellow for inconclusive, green for improvement.
Maya Patel: Ops ask: if alert fires, attach a small diagnostic bundle: top routes, offending requests sample, and recent deploys. That'd speed triage.
Jorge Ramos: Agree. Also add annotation layer for rollouts so we can correlate anomalies with config changes.
Maya Patel: OK, action items:
- Sasha: prototype CI + insufficient-data UI (ETA 3d)
- Ben: alert integration + query link (ETA 4d)
- Jorge: cohort/matching plan doc (ETA EOW)
- Maya: mockups for drilldown panel (ETA 2d)
I'll sync with PMs on thresholds.
Sasha Li: noted :+1:
Ben Carter: ping me when infra changes are needed.
Maya Patel: thanks all — will follow up with a short RFC.

title: #eng-platform: 1719023456
preview:
Priya Rao: Hey — ran into a weird charge on staging: client reconnects after EOF while a toolcall was in-flight, and the ledger shows both the prefix and the truncated toolcall counted twice.
Ben Carter: logs?
Priya Rao: pasted minimal trace:
```json
{"session_id":"s-abc123","route":"r-1","event":"toolcall","tokens_charged":42}
{"session_id":"s-abc123","route":"r-2","event":"reconnect","tokens_charged":42}
```
Marco Li: so route switched during reconnect? that could re-emit the toolcall accounting
Li Zhang: this is the case we discussed where router handoff doesn't dedupe provisional credits
Priya Rao: repro steps: start streaming -> send toolcall -> induce EOF on network -> client reconnects to different route -> resume -> toolcall truncated => double charge
Ben Carter: is the double charge provisional vs confirmed or both confirmed?
Priya Rao: both confirmed in ledger, UI shows two separate entries
deploy-bot: [alert] Routing handoff detected for session s-abc123 at 1719023460
Console Team: we should show provisional/confirmed grouping better in console (UX: group by session id + toolcall id)
Marco Li: internal API debate: should router tag provisional tokens with original route id and refuse re-credit on resume if session token exists? that's safer but could block legit retries
Li Zhang: alternative: attach idempotency key to toolcalls at the serving edge and carry through resume metadata, dedupe at billing layer
Ben Carter: idempotency would require client changes (send id per toolcall). can we generate server-side and persist across handoffs? cross-node persistence adds latency
Priya Rao: for now, quick mitigation: when route changes on resume, mark resumed toolcall as provisional and hold confirmation until session reconciles with original route or a timeout
Marco Li: agree. set hold window to 30s and surface a console flag for ops to force-commit if needed
Console Team: will add a telemetry panel: sessions with handoff + provisional credits > 0. show counts, session ids, routes
Ben Carter: also need routing policy tweak: prefer sticky affinity during in-flight toolcalls to avoid handoffs
Li Zhang: + add an audit event for billing dedupe decisions so we can debug retroactively
Priya Rao: action items:
- Marco: implement provisional hold on resume (PR + tests) (ETA 2d)
- Li: add idempotency key prototype for toolcalls (spike) (ETA 3d)
- Console Team: telemetry panel + UI grouping (ETA 4d)
- Ben: routing affinity tweak proposal (ETA EOD)
Priya Rao: cc @eng-sre in case we need to rollback routing changes :thumbsup:
Ben Carter: noted. I'll start a quick canary for affinity tweak on staging
Marco Li: thanks. if anyone has more repro logs pls drop them here
Li Zhang: dropping one extra trace from prod-redemo: https://internal/logs/s-abc123
Priya Rao: perfect — keeping this thread as source of truth. I'll open a postmortem ticket if we see customer impact
Marco Li: :raised_hands:

title: #eng-security: 1718888888
preview:
Maya Patel: Heads-up — we tightened keystore role scope in ap-southeast-1 this morning and I'm seeing a spike in mTLS handshake failures from proxy -> model-serving (~10% errors). Not sure if RBAC pruning cut off decrypt for a subset of replicas. Anyone seeing the same?
Arun Singh: saw the alert. noticed errors around 08:22 UTC. stack traces show KMS unwrap denies.
Priya Chen: thanks for flagging. can you paste the deny logs? KMS or proxy?
Maya Patel: logs: ```ERROR proxy: mTLS handshake fail: tls: failed to verify client certificate: x509: certificate signed by unknown authority; upstream unwrap error: AccessDenied: Permission denied for kms:Decrypt for key arn:aws:kms:ap-southeast-1:123456:key/abcd-efgh```
Ben Carter: that looks like IAM/KMS permission scope change. the role used by keystore-service was narrowed to granular arn/* earlier in the rollout. maybe we removed kms:Decrypt on the service account role.
Zoe Lin: deploying a fix now — rolling a policy patch to re-add kms:Decrypt for keystore-service-sa in ap-southeast-1. patch link: https://git.redwood/keystore/policy/changes/7689
Maya Patel: quick diagnostic commands for others: ```# from proxy pod kubectl exec -c proxy $POD -- openssl s_client -connect model-serving:443 -showcerts | head -n 50 kubectl logs proxy-$POD -c proxy | grep "mTLS handshake"```
Priya Chen: audit-bot: can you surface the list of IAM changes applied this morning in apac and which PRs touched keystore roles?
audit-bot: @Priya Chen found 3 IAM PRs merged between 07:50-08:15 UTC that touched keystore-service IAM bindings: PR#3421, PR#3427, PR#3430. Listing attached in workspace.
Arun Singh: for mitigations: 1) re-add kms:Decrypt on the role; 2) fallback to cached ephemeral key material on proxy to avoid immediate outage; 3) add a controlled rollback of the RBAC change if errors don't drop in 5m.
Ben Carter: preparing a rollback in case the policy patch doesn't converge. rolling policy will block other regions if we do a global change — ensure scope limited to ap-southeast-1.
Maya Patel: can infra-oncall confirm whether we saw any cert rotation events around the same time? suspect mismatch between certs and keystore permissions.
infra-oncall: checking cert-rotator logs... rotator ran at 08:10 UTC for several pods in ap-southeast-1 but completed with warning for 6 pods — "failed to persist key to keystore: access denied". looks correlated.
Zoe Lin: ok that aligns. rotator couldn't write new private keys because keystore write path requires kms:Encrypt + kms:Decrypt for verification. PR#3427 removed kms:Decrypt by mistake while narrowing write perms.
Maya Patel: priorities: (A) patch policy to restore decrypt for the rotator/keystore service in ap-southeast-1 (B) re-run cert rotator for failed pods after policy change (C) backfill audit logs and write incident note.
Priya Chen: I'm taking (C). I'll compile the list of affected pods + user-facing impact and open an incident doc.
Ben Carter: (A) done — policy patch applied to keystore-service-sa in ap-southeast-1 at 08:36 UTC via emergency verif. deploy-bot: ``deployed keystore-policy-rollback-apac v1.0.3``
deploy-bot: automatic canary checks: 5/5 proxies passed mTLS smoke test after policy patch.
Arun Singh: re-running rotator job for failed pods: `kubectl -n infra rollout restart deployment/cert-rotator` then `kubectl logs -f cert-rotator-...`
Zoe Lin: rotator completed re-issuance for the 6 pods at 08:58 UTC. metrics show handshake error rate dropped from 9.8% -> 0.6% in 15m window.
Maya Patel: excellent. next steps: 1) PR hygiene — require a KMS perms checklist for any keystore IAM changes; 2) add an enforcement rule in CI that runs a smoke mTLS call against canary after IAM changes touching keystore; 3) postmortem timeline.
Priya Chen: opening postmortem draft: https://docs.redwood/incidents/keystore-rbac-apac-1718888888 — I'll tag owners.
Ben Carter: also checking whether logs have missing audit entries for the failed writes — audit-bot any gaps?
audit-bot: there are 42 KMS Deny events between 08:10-08:25 UTC for keystore-service-sa in ap-southeast-1; all recorded. no lost audit entries, but we need retention export for compliance.
Maya Patel: thanks. tagging @Zoe Lin to own remediation PR for CI check and @Ben Carter to run follow-up to verify cross-region policy templates so we don't accidentally widen or over-narrow.
Zoe Lin: on it. will add a unit in the release checklist and create a lint rule to warn on removing kms:Decrypt from roles that are referenced by rotator/keystore.
Ben Carter: I'll coordinate with platform to add a canary that simulates keystore writes during IAM PR staged rollout.
Maya Patel: final note — if anyone still sees residual TLS errors post-09:15 UTC ping me, otherwise we'll mark incident as mitigated and keep postmortem open for action items. thanks all :heart:

title: #eng-ml: 1718249999
preview:
Priya Sinha: hey folks — seeing a weird CI pattern: when contributors push a quick follow-up commit the GH Actions -> Buildkite bridge sometimes spawns two eval runs that both upload artifacts named the same. one of the runners then errors trying to download the artifact (collision). anyone seen this?
Ben Carter: yep saw that last week. looks like webhook debounce + retry creates 2 workflow runs with different run_ids but same artifact path.
Sam Nguyen: we added a debounce in the GH side but Buildkite triggers can still be retried by the actions runner. retries + auto-retry for failed uploads = duplicate artifacts.
Priya Sinha: right — failure mode is: older job finishes upload later and overwrites the artifact while newer job already started pulling -> checksum mismatch and eval fails. not great for PR gating.
Luis Rodriguez: quick mitigation: include the workflow_run.id or run_id in the artifact name. e.g. `artifact-${{ github.run_id }}.tar.gz`. that avoids collisions but doesn't stop duplicate compute.
Anna Patel: also we should cancel earlier builds on new push. GitHub has `concurrency: group: ${{ github.ref }}` to cancel in-flight workflows. can we do that in our eval workflows?
Sam Nguyen: we tried `concurrency` but because the GH action merely enqueues a Buildkite job via API, the cancel doesn't kill the already-created buildkite build. need to wire cancellation to Buildkite too (API call).
Ben Carter: idea: GH action uses `concurrency` so only one workflow run is active, and in the `on: workflow_run` or post-step we call Buildkite API to cancel any builds with the same branch + older commit SHA.
Priya Sinha: sounds promising. but still worried about the race where both builds run long enough that both upload. can we make artifact upload idempotent?
Luis Rodriguez: we can have the runner upload to a `temp/<run_id>/` path and then do an atomic rename after successful checksum. rename will fail if destination exists — then uploader can backoff and abort. example: ```bash
TMP=artifacts/temp/$RUN_ID.tar.gz
FINAL=artifacts/$PR_NUMBER-latest.tar.gz
upload $TMP && if mv $TMP $FINAL; then echo ok; else echo already exists; fi
```
Anna Patel: +1 to atomic rename. Also we should surface which run is the authoritative one in PR checks. prefer showing the latest-run check as required.
Priya Sinha: on gating: can we use the Checks API to mark older evals as "skipped" and only count the latest? that way branch protection only considers latest evaluation.
Ben Carter: yes — we can post a check_run with `conclusion: neutral` for older ones and `conclusion: success/failure` for latest. need to ensure the integration has permissions and we don't hit rate limits.
Sam Nguyen: Buildkite also has an API to cancel builds; I'll add a step in the GH action that triggers cancel for any builds with `commit < older sha >` on same branch before creating new one.
Luis Rodriguez: volunteering to prototype the atomic upload + rename in the runner. will add a small helper in runner utils: `safe_upload(artifact, final_path)` and test with concurrent uploads.
Anna Patel: i'll update the GH workflow template to include `concurrency` and a post-step for Buildkite cancel + add a `GITHUB_RUN_ID` env var into the build metadata so builds are traceable.
ci-bot: automated note — PR #482: eval rerun requested by @maria. buildkite id 8453 canceled. :white_check_mark:
Priya Sinha: awesome. action items: Sam (GH cancel wiring), Luis (atomic upload PR), Anna (workflow update). I'll loop back once we have a prototype — thanks y'all :rocket:
Ben Carter: can we also add a small metric in Datadog for artifact collisions / checksum mismatches so we know if fixes reduce incidents?
Priya Sinha: yes pls — add `eval.artifact.collision` increment on any checksum mismatch. that'll help quantify impact.
Sam Nguyen: noted. I'll add a Datadog event when cancel API runs too.
Luis Rodriguez: starting branch `runner/safe-upload` now. will ping when ready for review.
Priya Sinha: sweet, closing the loop for now. if anyone sees new failures tag me. :pray:

Clara Evans: Please ensure the SDKs have the header change. If not, we should pin those orgs to v1 and notify support.
Diego Morales: Deploy bot scheduled at 17:00. I'm adding a preflight check for header mismatch counts.
deploy-bot: Started rollout: api-service v2 -> canary 10% (tag: v2-2024-04-03) :rocket:
ops-bot: alert: increased 95p latency on canary instances +120ms vs v1 at t+8m
Ben Carter: seeing error spike 500s on token-parse endpoint, sample log:
```
ERROR api-service token.parse failed client=web-frontend err="missing header X-RW-Client-Id"
```
Alice Nguyen: Hmm that's header fallout. Might be older SDKs still sending X-RW-Client. Can we add compatibility shim that accepts both headers while we notify clients?
Diego Morales: Patch would be quick; but we already started rollout. Options: rollback canary now, or pause and push compatibility shim to canary and continue.
Clara Evans: For enterprise customers, prefer pin to v1 immediately. Customer success oncall @sara will message affected customers.
Diego Morales: I propose: pause rollout, patch shim into v2 (accept X-RW-Client and X-RW-Client-Id), resume canary at 5% for 10m, then proceed.
Alice Nguyen: Agreed. Pause now and patch. @deploy-bot pause canary
deploy-bot: Pausing rollout for api-service v2 (canary stage). Reason: compatibility header errors. :pause_button:
Diego Morales: Pushing compatibility patch to v2-canary now. Command:
```
kubectl set image deploy/api-service api-service=registry.redwood/api-service:v2-compat-2024-04-03
```
ops-bot: canary health: rolling update in progress, 6/10 updated pods
Ben Carter: After patch, let's also add a temporary gateway rule to rewrite X-RW-Client -> X-RW-Client-Id for any traffic that lacks the new header.
Alice Nguyen: Good call. That minimizes risk while SDK updates roll out. Also create a changelog entry and send a short banner to customers: "v2 includes header rename; shim in place for 24h. Please update SDKs."
Clara Evans: I've created the support ticket template and pinned customers list: https://redwood.internal/tickets/INC-4521
Diego Morales: Resume canary to 5% when compatibility image hits pods.
deploy-bot: Canary resumed: api-service v2 -> 5% (image v2-compat-2024-04-03). Monitoring metrics: latency, 5xx, header-mismatch-rate.
ops-bot: metric update t+12m: 5xx back to baseline, header-mismatch-rate dropped 95%.
Alice Nguyen: Awesome. We'll hold at 5% for 30m, then 25% if stable. Plan to keep shim for 24 hours and then remove with a minor release once clients updated.
Ben Carter: Also add a feature flag to pin default to v1 for orgs marked in the enterprise list. I've created the flag: rollout.api-service.default_version (value: v1) for those org IDs.
Clara Evans: FYI support will send the banner + SDK PR links. CC @sara
Diego Morales: Noting rollback procedure in case of further regressions: rollback command:
```
deploy-bot rollback api-service --to-tag v1-2024-03-28
```
Alice Nguyen: Logging this thread in release notes and in the canary postmortem. Thanks team. :thumbsup:
sara (Support Oncall): Notified customers, CS acknowledged. Will escalate if any show errors.
deploy-bot: Canary completed step: stay at 5% for now. Next check at t+30m.
Alice Nguyen: Will post final status once we hit 25% or rollback. Keeping infra and support on standby.

title: #eng-releases: 1709123456
preview:
deploy-bot: Deploy completed — release v2.13.4 rolled to prod-usw2. Starting post-deploy quickcheck and sanity runbook.
Alice Nguyen: On it. I'll monitor p50/p95, errors, and kv-cache miss for 15m.
Ben Carter: Kicking off synthetic probes against /v1/generate and /v1/embed (100 reqs each).
QA Bot: Synth probes started at 1709123460 — summary after 30s: gen pass 96/100, embed pass 99/100, no 5xx.
SRE Oncall: Seeing p95 bump on gen-route: baseline 180ms -> now 220ms. Any recent config changes to batching or quantization?
Ben Carter: No config changes. Here's a quick perf snip from the probe runner:
```
route: /v1/generate p50: 48ms p95: 218ms p99: 450ms errors: 0 kv_miss_rate: 12.6% -> baseline 6.0%
collector: warning: short reconnect at 1709123472
```
Marco Li: KV miss delta +6.6% suggests cache warmup issue. We also observed longer tail during first 10m after rollout in canary.
Alice Nguyen: Also noticed a telemetry gap 1709123470-1709123490 for token-level traces — looks like collector restarted during deploy.
Ben Carter: Confirmed collector pod restarted; logs show 'collector: re-init succeeded' at 1709123475. Partial trace ingestion for that window.
SRE Oncall: Given minor latency bump + KV miss and telemetry gap, recommendation: PAUSE traffic ramp at 50% and observe 30m. Rollback if p95 or errors continue to rise.
Alice Nguyen: Pausing ramp now -> executing traffic-shift command.
Ben Carter: Command:
```
redwoodctl traffic shift --release v2.13.4 --region prod-usw2 --percent 50
```
deploy-bot: Traffic ramp updated: v2.13.4 = 50% in prod-usw2. :white_check_mark:
QA Bot: Re-running synth probes after ramp pause — 60s in: gen pass 99/100, embed pass 100/100, p95 now 192ms, kv_miss_rate ~11.5%.
SRE Oncall: Good. Leave ramp pinned at 50% for 30m. Create an infra ticket for collector instability and a follow-up to tune KV warmup policy.
Marco Li: Filed INF-4827: collector restart during deploy + KV warmup tracking. Assigned to infra. Notes attached.
Alice Nguyen: I'll follow up in 30m and unpause ramp to 100% only if p95 and kv_miss trending down. Tagging @oncall for alert thresholds.
SRE Oncall: Thanks. If anything exceeds p95>250ms or errors>0.5% escalate immediately. Otherwise hold.
Ben Carter: Adding a short note to release changelog: 'Deployed v2.13.4. Observed transient collector restart causing partial telemetry gap; ramp pinned at 50% pending infra follow-up.'
Alice Nguyen: OK. Closing thread for now — will post update at /update 30m. :hourglass_flowing_sand:
